verses.text: Jehu the seer meets the returning king: 'shouldest thou help the ungodly, and love them that hate the LORD?' Yet he adds that good things are found in him. Even God's servants are rebuked for ungodly friendships. Mercy tempers the reproof, naming the good while it warns of the wrong.

Jehu the seer meets the returning king: 'shouldest thou help the ungodly, and love them that hate the LORD?' Yet he adds that good things are found in him. Even God's servants are rebuked for ungodly friendships. Mercy tempers the reproof, naming the good while it warns of the wrong.

Jehu the seer meets the returning king: 'shouldest thou help the ungodly, and love them that hate the LORD?' Yet he adds that good things are found in him. Even God's servants are rebuked for ungodly friendships. Mercy tempers the reproof, naming the good while it warns of the wrong.

Jehoshaphat sets judges through the land, charging them to judge 'not for man, but for the LORD,' with the fear of God and no respect of persons or bribes. Justice is a sacred trust held under God's eye. The judge who serves the LORD will not be bought or swayed.

Jehoshaphat sets judges through the land, charging them to judge 'not for man, but for the LORD,' with the fear of God and no respect of persons or bribes. Justice is a sacred trust held under God's eye. The judge who serves the LORD will not be bought or swayed.

In Jerusalem he appoints Levites and priests for the LORD's matters, urging them to act faithfully and 'deal courageously, and the LORD shall be with the good.' Faithful service needs courage as well as care. God stands with those who do right boldly and without fear.
